intelligence.hypothesis: Insufficient telemetry; awaiting next valid capture.

vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is largely non-representative of the current visual data. P1 (String of Nickels), P2 (Mexican Mint), and P4 (Silver Guest) are not visible as established specimens. P3 (Pothos) is present but appears to be in a state of severe decline or total loss of foliage, represented only by the anchor.

inventory_reconciliation.P1: Systemic Loss: No plant material detected in the yellow pot.
inventory_reconciliation.P2: Systemic Loss: No plant material detected in the black pot.
inventory_reconciliation.P3: Systemic Loss/Critical: Only the white rabbit anchor remains; no viable Pothos foliage detected.
inventory_reconciliation.P4: Systemic Loss: No plant material detected in the black pot.

plant_audit.P3: The white rabbit anchor is present, but the Pothos is absent.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Soil appears dark and potentially over-saturated or compacted.
biome_observations.debris: White crystalline fragments (likely perlite or mineral salt buildup) are present on the surface of the central pot.
biome_observations.incidental_growth: A single, thin, chlorotic green sprout is visible in the central pot, likely an opportunistic weed or a failed germination attempt.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_t3: Initial state shows a single green sprout in the central pot.
temporal_deltas.t3_to_t2: Sprout remains static; no growth or movement observed.
temporal_deltas.t2_to_t1: Sprout shows slight postural shift, possibly due to phototropism or wilting.
temporal_deltas.t1_to_current: Sprout appears more upright but remains isolated; no other biological activity.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is in a state of total collapse. The lack of established foliage in all pots suggests a failure of the indoor environment to support the registered species. The single sprout is the only sign of life, but it lacks the vigor of a healthy specimen.
vision_report.anomalies: The presence of white crystalline debris suggests potential soil salinity issues or degradation of the potting medium. The total absence of the registered plants (P1-P4) indicates a catastrophic failure of the botanical collection.
vision_report.narrative_description: The audit reveals a desolate botanical environment. The registry is entirely disconnected from the physical reality of the desk, which currently holds only empty pots and a single, struggling sprout. The white rabbit anchor in P3 serves as a marker for a plant that is no longer there. The environment is essentially a graveyard for the original specimens.
